mission.title: Install and Configure k0s (Zero Friction Kubernetes)
mission.description: k0s is a lightweight, all-inclusive Kubernetes distribution designed for simplicity and ease of use. It is ideal for users looking to quickly bootstrap Kubernetes clusters on various environments, including cloud, bare metal, and edge devices. The key trade-off is that while it simplifies installation, it may not provide the extensive configurability of more complex distributions.

steps.title: Install the k0s binary
steps.description:
This step downloads and installs the k0s binary, which is required to create and manage your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -sSL https://github.com/k0sproject/k0s/releases/download/v1.31.0+k0s.0/k0s-v1.31.0+k0s.0-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/k0s

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/k0s

```

> Download the k0s binary.
> Make the k0s binary executable.

steps.title: Bootstrap the k0s controller
steps.description:
This command initializes the k0s controller, which is the control plane of your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

k0s bootstrap

```

> This command sets up the control plane.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

k0s kubeconfig admin > ~/.kube/config

```

> Generate kubeconfig for admin access.

steps.title: Verify the cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster.

steps.title: Post-installation setup
steps.description:
This step includes setting up monitoring and autoscaling for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/k0sproject/k0s/master/examples/monitoring.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/k0sproject/k0s/master/examples/autoscaling.yaml

```

> Deploy monitoring tools.
> Deploy autoscaling configuration.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description: Be aware that running a Kubernetes cluster may incur costs based on the cloud provider's pricing model.

uninstall.title: Remove the k0s cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the k0s cluster and all associated resources. Be cautious as this may lead to data loss.

```bash

k0s stop

rm -rf ~/.kube/config

```

> Stop the k0s controller.
> Remove kubeconfig file.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the k0s cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your cluster configuration and data. This process includes draining nodes, upgrading, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

curl -sSL https://github.com/k0sproject/k0s/releases/download/v1.31.0+k0s.0/k0s-v1.31.0+k0s.0-linux-amd64 -o /usr/local/bin/k0s

k0s upgrade

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get nodes

```

> Drain the node before upgrade.
> Download the new version of k0s.
> Upgrade the k0s cluster.
> Uncordon the node after upgrade.
> Verify that all nodes are ready.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k0s: command not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The k0s binary is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Ensure the k0s binary is installed and located in /usr/local/bin.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file may not be set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Regenerate the kubeconfig file using 'k0s kubeconfig admin > ~/.kube/config'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'node not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The node may not have joined the cluster properly.

**Fix:**
Check the node logs and ensure the k0s controller is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k0s controller is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The k0s controller may have failed to start.

**Fix:**
Restart the k0s controller using 'k0s start'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for multi-node clusters and improved network plugin integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old single-node setup method.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to migrate your configurations to the new multi-node setup.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum requirements for running k0s.
